title: MATRIYA Engine Contract
description: STABLE v1.0. Manifest that every Industrial Intelligence Platform engine MUST publish — a self-describing declaration, NOT runtime code and NOT an orchestrator. An engine that validates against this schema can be composed as a plug-and-play module. v1.0 = v0.3 (evidential/inductive reasoning classes; retrySafe/idempotencyKey; the outputEpistemics hypothesis safety envelope) plus two final decisions: (D1) change.recordsProducedArtifact — the produced artifact and the appended artifact may be one and the same; (D2) reproducibility — deterministic:false engines must declare whether/how a seed makes runs repeatable. GOVERNING RULE: the contract does not weaken to fit an engine — if an engine cannot be represented without relaxing its safety boundaries, that is a contract failure to REPORT, not an engine to force in. Documentation only; no runtime behaviour is implied or changed. This standard is frozen; future engines conform to it rather than the reverse.

apiVersion.description: Contract format version. Pins the manifest to a schema revision.

name.description: Stable, unique engine id (kebab-case, dot-namespaced allowed).

purpose.description: One sentence: what this engine does.

category.description: Informational grouping (search, comparison, recommendation, visualization, ...). Free text; never used for wiring.

stateless.description: MUST be true. An engine is Input -> Output and retains no per-call state and no knowledge of who called it or what runs next. Infra memoization (connection pools, model caches) is NOT engine state.

deterministic.description: Whether identical input + identical knowledge-base snapshot yields identical output. Search is approximately deterministic (ANN index ordering may vary).

sideEffects.description: Effect on the world. Reasoning/search engines should be 'none' or 'read'. 'write' engines must document what they mutate.

purity.description: Pure engines only read; stateful engines mutate shared state. Lets the Composer classify an engine WITHOUT reading its code. Must be consistent with `changes`: 'pure' iff `changes` is empty.

changes.description: What shared state this engine mutates. Empty array = pure/read-only. Same confidence number from a pure vs a stateful engine has very different operational meaning; the Composer must know which is which.

retrySafe.description: v0.3 F2. Whether the Composer may safely retry this engine after a failure/timeout without harmful duplication. Pure engines are trivially retry-safe. A STATEFUL engine claiming retrySafe:true MUST declare `idempotencyKey` (schema rule below) — otherwise a retried append would duplicate its effect.

idempotencyKey.description: v0.3 F2. How repeated calls with the same input are de-duplicated so a retry does not double-apply the effect (e.g. 'hash(claim + sorted sourceIds)'). Required when purity=stateful and retrySafe=true.

reproducibility.description: v1.0 D2. For engines that are `deterministic: false` (sampling, ANN ordering, generative search): declares whether a seed makes runs repeatable. Required when deterministic=false (schema rule below).

seeded.description: True if supplying a seed makes the output repeatable.

seedParam.description: Which input field carries the seed (e.g. 'constraints.seed'), or null if the engine is not seedable.

transformation.description: An engine is defined by what it TRANSFORMS, not by what it is. Declares Input -> Transformation -> Output so the platform is a set of transformations, not a bag of services.

statement.description: Human phrasing, e.g. 'Query -> ranked[Record] by semantic proximity'.

operation.description: Short verb id for the transform, e.g. 'rank_by_similarity', 'derive_landscape', 'append_event'.

reasoning.description: The 'quality of thinking'. Two engines can both emit confidence 0.81 while meaning entirely different things (semantic similarity vs. three independent causal evidences). This block declares what the confidence MEANS.

class.description: The kind of reasoning the engine performs. (v0.3 F1: added 'evidential' and 'inductive' — evidence qualification is neither retrieval nor pure causation.)

confidenceType.description: What the confidence number is derived from — its meaning, not its magnitude. (v0.3 F4: added 'mechanism_plausibility' and 'predictive_estimate' so a generative prior is never read as a measured probability.)

emitsTrace.description: Whether the engine can emit a reasoning_trace at runtime (in the result envelope).

evidenceModel.description: How evidence is counted for this engine (e.g. 'one hit = one evidence', 'independent sources only').

capabilities.description: Capability Vector — the cognitive capabilities the engine provides, each rated 0..5. Lets the Composer select engines by CAPABILITY, not by name (critical at 40-60+ engines). Maps to the researcher's See/Understand/Predict/Decide loop: observe->See, explain->Understand, predict->Predict, recommend/validate->Decide.

outputEpistemics.description: v0.3 F3 — the hypothesis safety envelope. Declares the epistemic status of outputs so a Composer knows, WITHOUT parsing payload schemas, whether an engine emits facts or unvalidated hypotheses. Invariant (enforced by schema rules below): a generative engine's outputs are ALWAYS 'hypothesis'; a 'hypothesis' output is ALWAYS human-gated and unvalidated and carries safety guarantees. Generated candidate = hypothesis awaiting validation — never knowledge, never recommendation.

outputClass.description: 'asserted' = returns knowledge as already stored, with its own provenance/status (retrieval); 'hypothesis' = produces NEW unvalidated claims (generation, evidence qualification); 'mixed'.

guarantees.description: Per-output safety fields the engine guarantees to attach. MUST be non-empty and include 'provenance' when outputClass=hypothesis.

neverAssertsAs.description: What the output must NEVER be presented as. Generative engines must include 'fact' and 'recommendation'.

validationGating.description: How outputs become validated. Hypotheses MUST be 'human_gated' (Principle 4).

consumes.description: Typed inputs. The Composer wires engines by matching a producer's `produces[].type` to a consumer's `consumes[].type`.

produces.description: Typed outputs (usually one). Each output is delivered inside the standard result envelope (see EngineContract.md).

confidence.description: How this engine expresses confidence in its output.

emits.description: Whether outputs carry a confidence value.

scale.description: Confidence scale. '0..1' is the platform default.

granularity.description: Where confidence attaches: per produced item, per result set, or both.

method.description: How confidence is derived (e.g. 'cosine_similarity', 'source_tier', 'model_prob'). Names its meaning so downstream can calibrate.

calibrated.description: True only if the value is a calibrated probability, not a raw score.

cost.description: Declared cost envelope so the Composer can plan.

estimate.description: Typical cost per invocation in `unit`. May be null if unmeasured.

latency.description: Declared latency envelope. Values MAY be null until measured.

dependencies.description: External resources the engine needs. NEVER another engine's code (statelessness) — data stores, indexes, models, host services only.

failureModes.description: Enumerated, stable failure codes and what the engine returns for each. A compliant engine fails as data (typed), not as an unhandled exception.

provenance.description: How outputs preserve provenance (platform Principle: no orphan knowledge).

domainSeparation.description: Which knowledge domain(s) the engine reads/writes; must respect external/internal/hypothesis separation.

allOf.$comment: v0.3 F2 — a stateful engine that claims retry-safety MUST declare how it de-duplicates (idempotencyKey). Otherwise a retried effect would double-apply.

v0.3 F3 — a GENERATIVE engine's outputs are ALWAYS hypotheses. A generative manifest that declares outputClass 'asserted'/'mixed' FAILS validation. This is the structural form of the rule: the contract does not weaken to present invented candidates as fact; it reports the failure.
v0.3 F3 — any 'hypothesis' output is unvalidated, human-gated, and carries safety guarantees including provenance.
v1.0 D2 — a non-deterministic engine must declare its reproducibility (whether a seed makes runs repeatable), so the Composer/audit trail is never silently unrepeatable.

domain.description: Which knowledge domain the change lands in (separation rule).

recordsProducedArtifact.description: v1.0 D1. True when this change persists the SAME logical artifact the engine returns in `produces` (e.g. the Knowledge Event is both returned and appended). Lets the Composer know the append is not a second, hidden output.

type.description: Named type id used for composition, e.g. 'ikl.SearchQuery@1'. Two ports connect only if their type ids match.

required.description: For inputs: whether the port must be supplied.

schema.description: Inline JSON Schema for the payload, OR use `schemaRef`.

schemaRef.description: URI of an external JSON Schema for the payload.

result.description: What the engine returns in this mode (e.g. 'empty result set', 'failed envelope with code').
